response:
Nurse Emily organized a creative writing workshop for the 24 residents in her care facility. She divided them into groups of 6, resulting in 4 groups in total. To ensure each group has enough writing prompts, she planned to allocate 3 prompts per group, which totals 12 prompts for the initial session. 

In addition, Emily prepared 10 extra prompts to be shared equally among all the groups. These prompts are stored in the library and will be distributed as needed. The library serves as a central resource for all writing materials, ensuring that every group has access to the necessary tools for creativity and expression.

Emily’s thoughtful preparation ensures that all residents have the opportunity to engage in the workshop, regardless of their writing experience. The library also includes backup materials in case of any unexpected needs during the session. 

The total number of writing prompts Emily needs to have prepared is 12 initial prompts plus 10 extra prompts, making a total of 22 prompts. 

The END.
